description:
Advances: specled.spec_review

Deliverable:
Extend SpecLedEx.Review.build_view/3 with (a) differential findings: read the base
ref's committed verification state via `git -C <root> show <base>:.spec/state.json`,
diff its findings against head findings, and expose introduced / resolved /
pre_existing lists plus a change_verdict on the view-model. Missing or unparseable
base state must yield an explicitly-flagged non-differential fallback
(delta_available?: false) — never misattribute. (b) Queue grouping data: per-subject
change kind (:spec_edited / :code_only / :impacted_only), per-subject diffstat
(adds/dels from code_changes), and a queue-ordered subjects list (grouped by kind,
sized descending). Renderer consumes these fields in S2 — this stage does NOT touch
the renderer.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix test test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs (new; flip
  the spec-verification stub for specled.spec_review.findings_delta to execute: true),
  mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs (no regressions)
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios specled.spec_review.findings_delta_classifies
  and specled.spec_review.findings_delta_missing_base

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
- priv/spec_review_assets/**
- lib/specled_ex/verifier*.ex
Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/review.ex
- lib/specled_ex/review/*.ex (new module for delta OK, e.g. findings_delta.ex)
- test/specled_ex/review_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs
- .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md (verification stub flip only)
- .spec/state.json (regenerated)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No renderer/DOM changes — the view-model contract is the deliverable
- No change to verification semantics or finding generation; base findings are read
  from committed state, never recomputed by re-running the verifier at base
- No new external dependencies

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: 3277fdc
Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/review.ex (build_view wiring: findings_delta, change_kind/2, diffstat, queue_subjects)
- test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md (stub flip only)
- .spec/state.json (regenerated by mix spec.check)
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Review.FindingsDeltaTest — 16 tests across:
  classify/3 differential (introduced/pre_existing/resolved, cross-shape identity, empty base),
  classify/3 fallback (absent base, unparseable base, nil ref),
  build_view integration (differential attach, non-differential fallback),
  Review.change_kind/2 (all three kinds), and queue grouping (change_kind, diffstat, queue ordering by group then size).
Verification stubs flipped: specled.spec_review.findings_delta (mix test test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs → execute: true)

Summary: Added SpecLedEx.Review.FindingsDelta, which reads the base ref's committed
verification state via `git show <base>:.spec/state.json`, diffs its findings against
head findings on a (code, entity, file, message) identity that spans the normalized
base shape and the live verifier shape, and exposes introduced/resolved/pre_existing
lists plus a change_verdict driven by introduced findings only. A missing or unparseable
base state yields delta_available?: false with empty lists so nothing is misattributed as
introduced. build_view/3 now also carries per-subject change_kind and diffstat plus a
queue-ordered queue_subjects list (grouped spec_edited → code_only → impacted_only, sized
descending, id tiebreak). Renderer/DOM untouched per Out of Scope; base findings are read
from committed state, never recomputed at base.

Scope/gate notes for verifier+auditor:
- Diff touches only Allowed globs. html.ex, priv/spec_review_assets/**, and verifier*.ex untouched.
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean. mix format --check-formatted: clean. mix deps.unlock --check-unused: clean.
- mix test (full): 904 tests, 0 failures. Targeted findings_delta_test (16) + review_test (23): green.
- mix spec.check: errors=0. My change RESOLVED the pre-existing review.ex→findings_delta finding and introduced zero new findings.
  The remaining 6 warnings are all pre-existing verification_target_missing_reference on lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex
  for S2/S3 requirements (change_scoped_overview, coverage_pivot_touched_first, findings_digest_dedup,
  repo_state_health_pane, review_queue_navigation, theme_tokens) — html.ex is explicitly Out of Scope for S1 and those
  stubs (html_layout_test.exs) are execute: false until S2/S3. These predate this commit (see HEAD~1:.spec/state.json).
- One transient flake observed on first spec.check run: specled.compiler_tracer tracer_test.exs:241 in the merged
  verification command (passes in isolation and on re-run; errors=0 second run). This is the known flaky
  full-suite/merged-run failure (ticket specled_-f98), unrelated to review code.

VERIFY: PASS

Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 5 (.spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md, .spec/state.json, lib/specled_ex/review.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex, test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (html.ex, priv/spec_review_assets/**, verifier*.ex all untouched)
- Spec file diff is a pure stub flip (execute: false->true for findings_delta_test), matching the "verification stub flip only" allowance.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (mix test test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs), passed: 1
  Regression: mix test test/specled_ex/review_test.exs green. Combined 39 tests, 0 failures.
- Requirements coverage:
  - specled.spec_review.findings_delta: COVERED.
    - introduced/pre_existing: findings_delta_test.exs:37 (asserts introduced["message"]=="brand new finding", pre_existing["message"]==f1_msg). Fails if the split misclassifies a head-only finding.
    - resolved: findings_delta_test.exs:91 (asserts [resolved]=result.resolved). Fails if base-only findings are dropped instead of marked resolved.
    - verdict driven by introduced only: findings_delta_test.exs:64-67 + :105 (introduced_count==1, by_severity==%{"error"=>1}; clean?==true when only resolved). Fails if verdict counted pre_existing.
    - cross-shape identity (base entity_id/level vs head subject_id/severity): findings_delta_test.exs:71 (asserts introduced==[] when the only finding matches across shapes). Fails if signature keyed on one shape's id only. I independently traced this: normalize_findings/1 (lib/specled_ex.ex:215-227) maps head subject_id->entity_id, severity->level while keeping code/file/message; verifier finding/5 (verifier.ex:2756) emits subject_id/severity/file/message/code; committed state.json findings carry code/entity_id/file/level/message. The signature (code, subject_id||entity_id, file, message) reconstructs the same tuple from both real shapes -- not just the hand-built test fixtures.
    - missing/unparseable/nil base fallback, never misattribute: findings_delta_test.exs:124/144/158 (delta_available?==false, base_reason set, introduced==[]). Fails if a fallback presented findings as introduced. build_view integration at :188 confirms real path falls back with introduced==[].
  - specled.spec_review.review_queue_navigation (DATA portion only -- rendering is S2, html.ex out of scope): change_kind/2 covered at findings_delta_test.exs:204-217; diffstat at :220-230; grouped+size-descending ordering at :254-337 (asserts exact id order ["big.subject","small.subject"] and kind order [:spec_edited,:code_only]). Fails if group order or size tiebreak regressed. The rendering half (finding indicator, chips, filter, URL fragment) is explicitly deferred to S2 per the Deliverable and is not in scope here.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. No renderer/DOM change (html.ex untouched). Base findings read via git show <base>:.spec/state.json (findings_delta.ex:121-128), never by re-running the verifier at base. No new deps (mix.exs/mix.lock unchanged; Jason pre-existing).
- Project verification (mix spec.check): exits non-zero, errors=0, warnings=6. See Notes -- does not change the verdict.
- Merge-gate side checks: mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix format --check-formatted clean; mix deps.unlock --check-unused clean.

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory; do NOT change the verdict):
- mix spec.check returns non-zero on 6 warnings, ALL verification_target_missing_reference on lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex for S2/S3 requirements (change_scoped_overview, coverage_pivot_touched_first, findings_digest_dedup, repo_state_health_pane, review_queue_navigation, theme_tokens). I confirmed against base ref 125b8dc's committed state.json that all 6 are PRE-EXISTING (present at base). html.ex is explicitly in this ticket's Out of Scope (files); those requirements are the S2 renderer's job. This change RESOLVED the one finding it was scoped to fix -- the findings_delta verification-target reference on review.ex (present at base, gone at head, via the `# covers: specled.spec_review.findings_delta` marker at review.ex:119) -- and INTRODUCED zero findings. A zero-warning spec.check at S1 is impossible without editing the out-of-scope html.ex, so the non-zero exit is the intended state of this stage, not a regression.
- Base state.json at 125b8dc also carried a realized_by_redundant_dup finding on realized_by.spec.md that is absent at head; the diff does not touch realized_by.spec.md, so this reflects a stale committed base artifact, not anything attributable to this ticket.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/b4c28666-8ce9-4a61-922a-352ab59212b4.jsonl

Reasoning: The one must-requirement advanced here, specled.spec_review.findings_delta, is
covered by load-bearing tests, and the provenance claim holds against real code (not just
fixtures). I independently traced the cross-shape identity: verifier finding/5
(verifier.ex:2755) emits subject_id/severity/file/message/code; normalize_findings
(specled_ex.ex:215-227) writes entity_id/level/file/message to committed state.json; the
signature (code, subject_id||entity_id, file, message) at findings_delta.ex:70-77
reconstructs the same tuple from both real shapes. Call site confirmed: review.ex:125 feeds
head findings (raw verifier report ++ synthetic) with analysis.base into classify; base is
read via git show <base>:.spec/state.json (findings_delta.ex:121-128), never recomputed.
Tests that would fail if the requirement broke: introduced/pre_existing split
findings_delta_test.exs:56-60; cross-shape identity :71-88 (base entity_id vs head subject_id,
asserts introduced==[]); resolved :102; verdict-from-introduced-only :64-67/:105; and the
three fallback cases :123-164 (delta_available?==false, introduced==[]) plus build_view
integration :188-201 -- all load-bearing.

Non-zero mix spec.check exit independently confirmed legitimate: I diffed base ref 125b8dc
committed state.json against head 3277fdc. Head carries 6 findings, all
verification_target_missing_reference naming target lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex for the S2/S3
requirements change_scoped_overview, coverage_pivot_touched_first, findings_digest_dedup,
repo_state_health_pane, review_queue_navigation, theme_tokens -- all present at base, 0 errors.
The diff resolved exactly one (the review.ex->findings_delta target, via the # covers: marker
at review.ex:119) and introduced zero. html.ex is Out of Scope for S1 and is untouched by the
diff, so the non-zero exit is the intended state of this stage, not a regression. (The
base-only realized_by_redundant_dup finding that also disappears is a stale committed-base
artifact -- the diff does not touch realized_by.spec.md -- not attributable here.)

Scope counts re-verified mechanically: diff is exactly 5 files
(.spec/specs/spec_review.spec.md [pure execute:false->true stub flip], .spec/state.json,
lib/specled_ex/review.ex, lib/specled_ex/review/findings_delta.ex,
test/specled_ex/review/findings_delta_test.exs) -- all in Allowed globs, 0 matching
Out-of-Scope globs (html.ex, priv/spec_review_assets/**, verifier*.ex untouched). No
renderer/DOM change; no new deps (mix.exs/mix.lock unchanged). Ran mix test on
findings_delta_test.exs + review_test.exs: 39 tests, 0 failures.

Non-blocking note (not a gap in this ticket contract): the synthetic no_realized_by
detector_unavailable findings are synthesized by the review view-model (review.ex:493) and are
never part of the committed verifier state.json, so a pre-existing no_realized_by condition on
an affected subject always classifies as introduced (the base set never contains it). This is
consistent with findings_delta literal contract (present at head, absent at base) and is
exercised deliberately by the integration test at :175-186, but it means a PR that merely
touches an already-unbound subject can read as introducing a finding. Flag for S2/S3
verdict-presentation work (triage_panel / no_realized_by), not a defect in this stage.
APPROVED. Merged into specled_-7md/spec-review-redesign. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: S1: View-model — findings delta + queue grouping data
